name: A Tribe Called Quest
description: Jazz-rap pioneers. Can I Kick It? Yes you can.

name: Alice in Chains
description: Layne Staley and Jerry Cantrell. Harmonies from the abyss.

name: Allman Brothers Band
description: Duane and Gregg. At Fillmore East. Southern blues rock perfection.

name: Amy Winehouse
description: Back to Black. Voice from another era trapped in this one.

name: Antonio Carlos Jobim
description: The Girl from Ipanema. Brazilian genius who changed jazz.

name: Aphex Twin
description: no clue

name: Arcade Fire
description: Montreal collective. Funeral. Arena indie before that was a thing.

name: Aretha Franklin
description: The Queen. Respect. That's it. That's the description.

name: B.B. King
description: Lucille. The thrill is gone. King of the Blues.

name: Backstreet Boys
description: I Want It That Way. Sold 100 million records.

name: Beastie Boys
description: Three white kids from NYC. Licensed to Ill to Paul's Boutique genius.

name: Bee Gees
description: Stayin' Alive. The Gibbs brothers owned Saturday night.

name: Beyoncé
description: Destiny's Child to solo dominance. Lemonade. Renaissance. The standard.

name: Billie Eilish
description: Bedroom pop to global domination. Whispered a generation into existence.

name: Blur
description: Damon Albarn's art school cool. Parklife to experimental.

name: Boards of Canada
description: Nostalgic electronica. Music Has the Right to Children. Haunted.

name: Bob Dylan
description: Timothy Chalamet

name: Bon Iver
description: Justin Vernon in a cabin. For Emma. Skinny Love. Then reinvented everything.

name: Brian Eno
description: Invented ambient music. Music for Airports. Producer to the stars.

name: Cat Stevens
description: Peace Train. Wild World. Tea for the Tillerman. Then walked away.

name: Charles Mingus
description: Composer, bandleader, force of nature. The bass as orchestra.

name: Chic
description: Nile Rodgers and Bernard Edwards. Le Freak. Good Times. The groove.

name: Chuck Berry
description: Invented the rock guitar solo. Every riff traces back here.

name: Cream
description: Clapton, Baker, Bruce. Power trio. Crossroads. Sunshine of Your Love.

name: D'Angelo
description: Voodoo. Brown Sugar. Made R&B organic and raw again.

name: Daft Punk
description: Around the World. Discovery. Random Access Memories. French robots.

name: David Bowie
description: Ziggy Stardust. Chameleon. Alien. The most important artist of the 70s.

name: Depeche Mode
description: Basildon boys who made darkness danceable. Never stopped.

name: Derrick May
description: Detroit techno originator. Strings of Life. Innovator.

name: Dick Dale
description: King of the Surf Guitar. Misirlou. That reverb.

name: Disclosure
description: UK brothers. Settle. Brought house to the streaming generation.

name: Donna Summer
description: Queen of Disco. I Feel Love. Moroder's synths + her voice.

name: Drake
description: Started from the bottom. Singing rapper. Dominated streaming.

name: Earth, Wind & Fire
description: September. Boogie Wonderland. Joy as a musical philosophy.

name: Elton John
description: Tiny Dancer. Rocket Man. Spectacles and sequins over pure songcraft.

name: Elvis Presley
description: The King. Took blues and country, made it dangerous, then safe, then strange again.

name: Eminem
description: Slim Shady. Marshall Mathers. Best-selling rapper ever. Detroit.

name: Erykah Badu
description: Baduizm. High priestess of neo-soul. Analog Girl in a Digital World.

name: Fleet Foxes
description: Robin Pecknold. Baroque folk harmonies from Seattle. Timeless.

name: Fleetwood Mac
description: Reinvented as pop-rock royalty. Rumours: heartbreak as art.

name: Flume
description: Australian future bass. Self-titled debut at 21.

name: Frank Ocean
description: Channel Orange. Blonde. Redefined vulnerability in R&B.

name: Frankie Knuckles
description: The Godfather of House. Chicago warehouse. Your Love.

name: Grandmaster Flash
description: The Message. The turntable as instrument. Hip hop's first masterpiece.

name: Green Day
description: Brought punk to the suburbs. Dookie to American Idiot.

name: Herbie Hancock
description: Head Hunters. Future Shock. Jazz that bumps.

name: Howlin' Wolf
description: That voice. Huge, terrifying, elemental.

name: Iron & Wine
description: Sam Beam's whisper. The Creek Drank the Cradle. Hushed beauty.

name: James Brown
description: The Godfather. Invented funk. The one.

name: James Taylor
description: Fire and Rain. Sweet Baby James. Laurel Canyon warmth.

name: Jay-Z
description: Hov. Reasonable Doubt to Blueprint to empire. Brooklyn's own.

name: Jefferson Airplane
description: San Francisco's flagship. Grace Slick's voice cut through the haze.

name: Jim Reeves
description: Gentleman Jim. Velvet voice, countrypolitan smooth.

name: Jimi Hendrix
description: Rewired the electric guitar. Seattle via London via another dimension.

name: Joan Baez
description: The voice of the movement. Pure, clear, committed.

name: John Coltrane
description: From hard bop through sheets of sound to spiritual transcendence.

name: John Mayall
description: The schoolmaster. Clapton, Peter Green, Mick Taylor all came through.

name: Johnny Cash
description: The Man in Black. Folsom Prison. American legend.

name: Joni Mitchell
description: Blue. Court and Spark. Painted in sound. Peerless.

name: Joy Division
description: Ian Curtis. Unknown Pleasures. Atmosphere. Post-punk's beating heart.

name: Kamasi Washington
description: The Epic. Brought jazz back to the conversation. Spiritual.

name: Kanye West
description: College Dropout. MBDTF. Yeezus. Genius producer turned complicated everything.

name: Kendrick Lamar
description: Good Kid, MAAD City. DAMN. Mr. Morale. Compton's prophet.

name: Kraftwerk
description: Autobahn. Trans-Europe Express. Düsseldorf robots who invented the future.

name: Lauryn Hill
description: Miseducation. One perfect solo album. That's enough.

name: Led Zeppelin
description: The hammer of the gods. Bonham, Page, Plant, Jones. Heavy.

name: Lesley Gore
description: It's My Party. Teen pop with backbone.

name: Madonna
description: Material Girl to icon. Reinvented herself every era.

name: Marvin Gaye
description: What's Going On. Let's Get It On. Soul with a conscience.

name: Michael Jackson
description: The King of Pop. Thriller. Moonwalk. Changed music, dance, video, everything.

name: Miles Davis
description: Cool incarnate. Reinvented jazz every decade.

name: Muddy Waters
description: Electrified the Delta. Every British kid wanted to be him.

name: Nas
description: Illmatic. Queensbridge poet. One perfect album and a career of great ones.

name: New Order
description: From the ashes of Joy Division. Blue Monday changed everything.

name: Nick Drake
description: Pink Moon. Three albums, no fame, then gone. Discovered too late.

name: Nirvana
description: Nevermind. Kurt Cobain screamed and whispered a generation awake.

name: Oasis
description: Gallagher brothers. Wonderwall. Definitely Maybe. Lads.

name: Ornette Coleman
description: Threw out the rulebook. Free Jazz wasn't a genre, it was a declaration.

name: Otis Redding
description: Stax's greatest voice. Raw, urgent, gone too soon.

name: OutKast
description: André 3000 and Big Boi. ATLiens. Stankonia. Hey Ya!

name: Parliament-Funkadelic
description: George Clinton's mothership. One nation under a groove.

name: Patsy Cline
description: Crazy. I Fall to Pieces. Country's greatest voice, gone too soon.

name: Pearl Jam
description: Eddie Vedder's baritone. Ten. Alive. Still going.

name: Pete Seeger
description: The link to Woody Guthrie. Keeper of the flame.

name: Pink Floyd
description: Dark Side. The Wall. Comfortably Numb. Art as stadium rock.

name: Prince
description: Purple Rain. Genius. Played every instrument. Danced harder than anyone.

name: Public Enemy
description: Fight the Power. Chuck D's voice, Flavor Flav's clock, Bomb Squad's noise.

name: Queen
description: Freddie Mercury's range. Brian May's guitar. Bohemian Rhapsody.

name: Radiohead
description: OK Computer. Kid A. Kept evolving past everyone.

name: Ramones
description: Queens. 1-2-3-4! Two-minute songs that launched a thousand bands.

name: Return to Forever
description: Chick Corea's electric group. Romantic Warrior. Virtuosity.

name: Robert Glasper
description: Black Radio. Jazz meets hip hop meets R&B. Genre-fluid.

name: Rolling Stones
description: The anti-Beatles. Blues, swagger, menace.

name: Run-DMC
description: Walk This Way. Made hip hop rock. Adidas with no laces.

name: Ryan Adams
description: Heartbreaker. Whiskeytown to prolific solo career.

name: Sam Cooke
description: Gospel smoothness turned secular. A Change Is Gonna Come.

name: Sex Pistols
description: Anarchy in the UK. One album. Changed everything.

name: Shabaka Hutchings
description: Sons of Kemet. The Comet Is Coming. London's jazz renaissance.

name: Simon & Garfunkel
description: The Sound of Silence. Poetry and harmony from Queens.

name: Skrillex
description: Scary Monsters. Brought dubstep to the mainstream.

name: Sly & The Family Stone
description: Integrated band, integrated sound. Everyday People.

name: Soundgarden
description: Chris Cornell's voice. Black Hole Sun. Heavy, strange, beautiful.

name: Spice Girls
description: Girl Power. Wannabe. Five personalities, one cultural earthquake.

name: Stan Getz
description: The Sound. Brought bossa nova to America.

name: Stevie Ray Vaughan
description: Texas Flood. Last great blues guitar hero. Breathtaking.

name: Stevie Wonder
description: Little Stevie became a genius. The 70s run is untouchable.

name: Sturgill Simpson
description: Metamodern Sounds. Country's psychedelic outlaw for a new era.

name: Sun Ra
description: From Saturn. Arkestra. Space is the place.

name: T. Rex
description: Marc Bolan. Electric Warrior. Invented glam rock over a weekend.

name: Talking Heads
description: David Byrne's big suit. Psycho Killer. Same as it ever was.

name: Tame Impala
description: Kevin Parker's one-man psych-pop universe. Currents changed the game.

name: Tangerine Dream
description: Berlin school. Sequencers and cosmic ambience.

name: Taylor Swift
description: Country kid turned pop titan turned indie. Eras Tour broke records.

name: Tears for Fears
description: Everybody Wants to Rule the World. Ambition as pop.

name: The Archies
description: Sugar Sugar. A cartoon band that outsold the Beatles in 1969.

name: The Beach Boys
description: California myth-makers. Brian Wilson's genius took them far beyond surfing.

name: The Beatles
description: Liverpool four-piece that rewrote the rules. Again. And again.

name: The Byrds
description: Jangle and shimmer. Dylan through a Rickenbacker.

name: The Chemical Brothers
description: Block Rockin' Beats. Big beat pioneers from Manchester.

name: The Clash
description: The only band that matters. Punk plus reggae plus everything.

name: The Cure
description: Robert Smith's lipstick and hair. Goth, pop, heartbreak, joy.

name: The Doors
description: Morrison's poetry, Manzarek's keys. LA noir meets Dionysus.

name: The Monkees
description: Manufactured for TV, became real. Hey Hey.

name: The Notorious B.I.G.
description: Ready to Die. Brooklyn's finest storyteller. Juicy.

name: The Prodigy
description: Firestarter. Breathe. Rave music with punk attitude.

name: The Ronettes
description: Be My Baby. Phil Spector's wall of sound, Ronnie's voice on top.

name: The Strokes
description: Is This It. NYC garage revival. Made rock cool again in 2001.

name: The Supremes
description: Diana Ross and Motown's crown jewels. Hit after hit.

name: The Temptations
description: My Girl. Five voices, perfect choreography, unstoppable.

name: The Weeknd
description: Abel Tesfaye. Dark R&B turned stadium pop. Blinding Lights.

name: The White Stripes
description: Jack and Meg. Two people, one guitar, one drum kit. Seven Nation Army.

name: The Who
description: Maximum R&B. Townshend's windmill, Moon's chaos.

name: Them
description: Belfast R&B. Van Morrison's raw start. Gloria.

name: Three 6 Mafia
description: Memphis. Dark, hypnotic, influential. Won an Oscar.

name: Travis Scott
description: Astroworld. Rage. Houston's psychedelic trap auteur.

name: Tupac Shakur
description: All Eyez on Me. Poet, activist, icon. California Love.

name: Tyler Childers
description: Purgatory. Kentucky coal country voice. Appalachian new guard.

name: Tyler, the Creator
description: Odd Future to IGOR. Constant evolution.

name: Waylon Jennings
description: Refused Nashville's polish. Did it his way.

name: Weather Report
description: Zawinul and Shorter. Birdland. Jazz fusion's greatest band.

name: Wilco
description: Jeff Tweedy. Uncle Tupelo to Yankee Hotel Foxtrot.

name: Willie Nelson
description: Red Headed Stranger. On the Road Again. Texas icon.

name: Wu-Tang Clan
description: 36 Chambers. Staten Island's finest. Protect Ya Neck.

name: ZZ Top
description: Texas boogie. La Grange. Sharp Dressed Man. Those beards.

name: 2Pac
description: All Eyez on Me. Me Against the World. Dear Mama. Shot twice, survived, made better music. Then gone at 25.

name: 50 Cent
description: Get Rich or Die Tryin'. Nine bullets and a smile. In Da Club is the greatest party song ever made.

name: Aaliyah
description: Age Ain't Nothing but a Number at 15. One in a Million redefined R&B. Try Again. Gone at 22. The blueprint nobody fully copied.

name: ABBA
description: Waterloo. Dancing Queen. Mamma Mia. Four Swedes in sequins who wrote the greatest pop songs ever made. Voyage proved they still could.

name: AC/DC
description: Two chords, maximum voltage. Bon Scott's swagger then Angus kept going. Rock reduced to its purest element.

name: Aerosmith
description: Dream On. Walk This Way. Toys in the Attic. Pump. Boston bad boys who survived everything including themselves.

name: Al Green
description: Let's Stay Together. Tired of Being Alone. Take Me to the River. The most sensual voice in soul music. Then he found God and made gospel.
eric_take: My father never had to compete w Al Green, Marvin Gaye & them dudes like we gotta do w Trey & them — Joe Budden (truth)

name: A★Teens
description: Swedish teens covering ABBA and making it work. Mamma Mia at the school dance. Upside Down was their own and it slapped. Y2K pop at its most joyful.

name: Black Sabbath
description: Invented heavy metal in a Birmingham factory town. Tony Iommi's tritone changed everything.

name: Bob Marley & The Wailers
description: Exodus. Redemption Song. Took reggae from Kingston to the entire world. The voice of liberation.

name: Bobby Darin
description: Mack the Knife. Beyond the Sea. Splish Splash. Rock and roll kid turned Vegas lounge king turned folk protest singer. Did everything, died at 37, left a catalog that won't quit.

name: Britney Spears
description: ...Baby One More Time. That schoolgirl outfit. Toxic is the greatest pop production of the 2000s. Blackout was ahead of its time by a decade. She didn't just define an era, she was the era.

name: Christina Aguilera
description: Genie in a Bottle, then she grew up and made Stripped. Beautiful. Fighter. That voice has four octaves and she uses every single one. Dirrty changed the conversation.

name: Common
description: I Used to Love H.E.R. is hip hop as a woman you're losing. Chicago's conscious voice. Be is a perfect album.
eric_take: commons album is one of the best ive heard all year

name: Debbie Gibson
description: Wrote, produced, and performed Only in My Dreams at 16. Lost in Your Eyes went #1. The original teen pop auteur before anyone used that word.

name: Demi Lovato
description: Heart Attack. Sorry Not Sorry. Disney kid who fought through everything and came out screaming. Cool for the Summer. That voice could shatter concrete.

name: DIR EN GREY
description: Visual kei to progressive metal to something nobody can name. Uroboros is a masterpiece that will terrify you. Kyo's vocals go places the human voice shouldn't.

name: Dream Theater
description: Pull Me Under. Metropolis Part 2 is a concept album that actually works. Portnoy, Petrucci, Rudess — prog-metal's holy trinity, time signatures for days.

name: Eric Clapton
description: Greatest guitarist in the world, CREAM, White Room

name: Faithless
description: Insomnia. God Is a DJ. Maxi Jazz preaching over house beats at 3 AM. Salva Mea in a field of 50,000 people and everyone's hands are up.

name: Gary Moore
description: Blues-rock titan who could make a Les Paul weep. Still Got the Blues is the benchmark. Parisienne Walkways at a pub in Dublin and you're done for the night.

name: Gorillaz
description: Damon Albarn's cartoon band that turned out to be the real thing. Feel Good Inc. Clint Eastwood. Genre is irrelevant.

name: Gucci Mane
description: Trap House to trap god. East Atlanta Santa. More mixtapes than anyone can count, and they all bump.
eric_take: why i am up at 220 am on a sunday night listening to gucci mane is beyond me

name: Hank Mobley
description: The middleweight champion of the tenor sax. Soul Station is one of the most perfect hard bop records ever pressed. Smooth enough to pour over ice.

name: Hilary Duff
description: So Yesterday. Come Clean. Lizzie McGuire's metamorphosis into a legit pop star. Dignity was underrated. Came back in 2026 and proved she never left.

name: James Blake
description: The Wilhelm Scream. That voice through the static. Took dubstep, stripped it to the bone, and made it weep. Limit to Your Love on a late night drive and the bass drops through the floor.

name: Jesse McCartney
description: Beautiful Soul. Leavin'. The voice behind Leona Lewis's Bleeding Love. Boy band kid turned genuine songwriter. Just So You Know still hits.

name: Jessica Simpson
description: I Wanna Love You Forever. The voice was always the real thing. With You. A Public Affair. Pop career, fashion empire, and a memoir that rewrote her story.

name: Joe Budden
description: Pump It Up was the hit. Mood Muzik was the art. Introspection over heavy beats, no filter.

name: Kate Bush
description: Wuthering Heights at 19. Running Up That Hill 37 years later. Built entire worlds in a home studio.

name: Lil Wayne
description: Weezy F Baby. Tha Carter series rewrote what a rapper could be. Punchlines, metaphors, ad-libs, chaos.
eric_take: I fall off my skateboard, I don't fall in love

name: Lupe Fiasco
description: Kick, Push. Food & Liquor. The Cool. Chicago's other genius — the one who reads books.

name: Mandy Moore
description: Candy at 15. Then she grew up, made Wild Hope, and became the quiet one with taste. Coverage is a covers album that works. Extraordinary is the sleeper.

name: Massive Attack
description: Bristol trip-hop. Blue Lines to Mezzanine. Teardrop, Angel, Unfinished Sympathy. Invented a mood.

name: Neil Young
description: Heart of Gold. Cortez the Killer. Rust Never Sleeps. Half a century of never doing the same thing twice.

name: Nina Simone
description: Classically trained, couldn't be contained. Piano and fury. Civil rights and heartbreak and Feeling Good.

name: Pixies
description: Loud quiet loud. Black Francis screaming, Kim Deal holding it down. Where Is My Mind started a revolution.

name: R.E.M.
description: Athens, Georgia. Murmur to Automatic. Invented college rock, then outgrew it. Losing My Religion.

name: RBD
description: Rebelde. Six Mexican kids from a telenovela who became the biggest Latin pop group of the 2000s. Ser o Parecer. Solo Quedate en Silencio. Estadio Azteca, sold out.

name: Red Hot Chili Peppers
description: californication all around the world, its all love

name: Rick Nelson
description: Hello Mary Lou. Travelin' Man. Teen idol who actually played guitar and meant it. Garden Party is the greatest I don't care what you think song ever recorded.

name: Rihanna
description: Umbrella to ANTI. Barbados to the top of everything. Eight albums in eight years, each one a different Rihanna.
eric_take: rihanna makes hits left and right holy shit

name: Robbie Williams
description: Take That's wildcard gone solo. Angels is the biggest pub singalong in British history. Feel is pure pop perfection. Owned the UK like nobody since.

name: Sarah Vaughan
description: The Divine One. A voice that could bend notes into shapes nobody else could find. Lullaby of Birdland is jazz vocals at the peak of the mountain.

name: Snoop Dogg
description: Gin and Juice. That drawl. Doggystyle dropped and the West Coast never sounded the same. From Long Beach to everything, forever.

name: Supergrass
description: Caught by the Fuzz at 17. Alright is the sound of being young and not caring. Pumping on Your Stereo. Britpop's most underrated band, full stop.

name: Suzi Quatro
description: The first female bass player to become a rock star. 48 Crash. Can the Can. Leather jumpsuit, no apologies. Joan Jett took notes.

name: Talk Talk
description: Started synth pop, ended post-rock. Spirit of Eden is the most important left turn in music history. Mark Hollis whispered and the world leaned in.

name: The Drifters
description: Under the Boardwalk. Save the Last Dance for Me. Up on the Roof. They wrote the soundtrack to every summer you wish you had.

name: The Jimi Hendrix Experience
description: Three albums. That's all it took. Purple Haze. Voodoo Child. All Along the Watchtower. The greatest guitarist who ever lived, gone at 27.

name: Thievery Corporation
description: DC's own downtempo ambassadors. Lebanese Blonde. The Mirror Conspiracy at 2 AM with the lights low. Dub, bossa nova, trip-hop — no borders, no genre, just vibes.

name: Tool
description: Lateralus. Fibonacci spirals and seven-minute time signatures. Maynard screams and you feel it in your spine. Thirteen years between albums and nobody stopped waiting.

name: Vanessa Hudgens
description: Say OK. Come Back to Me. High School Musical's Gabriella stepped out solo and the V album had more edge than anyone expected.

name: Waka Flocka Flame
description: Hard in da Paint. No Hands. Flockaveli is the purest distillation of go-dumb energy ever recorded.
eric_take: in the mood for some ignorant waka flocka shit

name: Bad Bunny
description: El Conejo Malo. Reggaeton to experimental. Puerto Rico's biggest global export.

name: 21 Savage
description: British passport, Atlanta trap. Savage Mode II with Metro Boomin is the blueprint. A lot is an understatement.

name: Adele
description: Named her albums after her age. 21 broke every record that existed. Rolling in the Deep is fury disguised as pop.

name: Alvvays
description: Canadian dream pop perfection. Archie, Marry Me is the hook that never leaves. Two v's, zero filler.

name: Arctic Monkeys
description: I Bet You Look Good on the Dancefloor. Sheffield lads to lounge lizards. Alex Turner reinvents himself every album.

name: ATEEZ
description: K-pop with pirate energy. Eight members, zero chill. Guerrilla and Wonderland go harder than they need to.

name: Bauhaus
description: Bela Lugosi's Dead. Nine minutes of gothic post-punk that invented an entire subculture. Peter Murphy's cheekbones did the rest.

name: black midi
description: London math rock chaos. Schlagenheim sounds like a car crash you can't stop replaying. Geordie Greep screams through jazz.

name: Boston
description: More Than a Feeling. Tom Scholz built a spaceship in his basement. The debut sold 17 million copies.

name: boygenius
description: Phoebe, Julien, Lucy. Three singer-songwriters who didn't need a supergroup but gave us one anyway.

name: Buddy Holly
description: Died at 22. Peggy Sue, That'll Be the Day, Everyday. Invented the rock band format. The plane crash that stopped the music.

name: Captain Beefheart
description: Don Van Vliet. Trout Mask Replica is either the greatest album ever made or unlistenable noise. No middle ground.

name: Caroline Polachek
description: Ex-Chairlift, solo siren. Bunny Is a Rider is hyperpop art school. That voice bends in ways voices shouldn't.

name: Central Cee
description: West London drill gone global. Doja is the crossover. UK rap's most exportable voice.

name: Chappell Roan
description: Midwest princess. Good Luck, Babe! is the queer pop anthem of the decade. Zero to stadiums in one album cycle.

name: Chicago
description: Jazz-rock with horns. 25 or 6 to 4 is the riff. Saturday in the Park is the hit. Then Peter Cetera turned them into a ballad machine.

name: Childish Gambino
description: Donald Glover rapping, singing, directing, writing. Because the Internet to Awaken My Love. Constant reinvention.
eric_take: i feel dirty for liking this childish gambino album

name: Dead Kennedys
description: Jello Biafra's satire was sharper than most punk's anger. Holiday in Cambodia. California Uber Alles. West Coast hardcore with a brain.

name: Destiny’s Child
description: Beyoncé's origin story. Say My Name. Bills, Bills, Bills. Survivor. Kelly and Michelle held their own. The group that launched a dynasty.

name: Dire Straits
description: Mark Knopfler's fingers. Sultans of Swing is the audition tape. Money for Nothing is the MTV anthem. Brothers in Arms sold 30 million.

name: Doja Cat
description: Mooo! was a meme. Say So was a TikTok smash. Planet Her proved she's an actual artist. Scarlet proved she doesn't care what you think.

name: Donovan
description: Britain's answer to Dylan. Sunshine Superman, Mellow Yellow, Hurdy Gurdy Man. Psychedelic folk before that was a genre.

name: Ed Sheeran
description: Loop pedal busker to biggest pop star on Earth. Shape of You. Thinking Out Loud. The math symbols keep coming.

name: Eurythmics
description: Sweet Dreams Are Made of This. Annie Lennox's voice over Dave Stewart's synths. Who am I to disagree.

name: Feid
description: Ferxxo. Medellín's reggaeton prince. FERXXOCALIPSIS is the party. Collaboration machine with Karol G and everyone else.

name: Fontaines D.C.
description: Dublin post-punk. Dogrel is poetry shouted over guitars. Boys in the Better Land is the anthem. Ireland's loudest export.

name: Franz Ferdinand
description: Take Me Out. That guitar riff change is the moment indie disco was born. Scottish art school groove.

name: Fugees
description: The Score. Lauryn Hill, Wyclef, Pras. Killing Me Softly reimagined. Ready or Not. One perfect album then implosion.

name: Ghost
description: Swedish metal dressed as satanic pop. Square Hammer is an arena anthem. Tobias Forge is the only constant behind the mask.

name: Gojira
description: French environmental metal. Flying Whales is the song. From Mars to Sirius is the album. Heavy as tectonic plates.

name: Grace Jones
description: Jamaican-American icon. Nightclubbing is art. Pull Up to the Bumper is funk. Slave to the Rhythm is eternal. Fashion, model, force of nature.

name: Grimes
description: Claire Boucher built Visions on GarageBand in two weeks. Oblivion is the song. Art Angels is the pop pivot. Cyberpunk before it was cringe.

name: Hozier
description: Take Me to Church. Irish folk-soul with a baritone that fills cathedrals. Too Sweet broke streaming records in 2024.

name: Ice Spice
description: Bronx drill princess. Munch was the moment. Y2K is the debut. Gen Z energy distilled into ad-libs.

name: Jack Harlow
description: Louisville charm. First Class sampled Fergie and went number one. Industry Baby with Lil Nas X. Frat rap grown up.

name: Janis Joplin
description: Me and Bobby McGee. Piece of My Heart. The voice that tore itself apart. Pearl came out posthumous. 27 forever.

name: Japanese Breakfast
description: Michelle Zauner turned grief into Psychopomp. Jubilee is joy after mourning. Also wrote Crying in H Mart. Polymath.

name: Juan Gabriel
description: El Divo de Juárez. Querida. Amor Eterno. Mexico's greatest songwriter. 42 albums. Performed for millions. Cried with all of them.

name: KAROL G
description: La Bichota. TQG with Shakira was the crossover. Mañana Será Bonito is the album. Colombia to the world.

name: Knocked Loose
description: Kentucky hardcore. Counting Worms is violence. You Won't Go Before You're Supposed To proved heavy music still moves.

name: Leonard Cohen
description: Hallelujah. Suzanne. Bird on the Wire. Canadian poet who sang in a baritone whisper. Wrote until the very end.

name: Lil Peep
description: Emo rap pioneer. Awful Things. Star Shopping. Gone at 21. Opened a door between punk and SoundCloud rap that never closed.

name: Magdalena Bay
description: Synth-pop duo. Mercurial World is a simulation. Imaginal Disk is the upgrade. Retro-futurism with a wink.

name: Maroon 5
description: Songs About Jane is a great debut. This Love, She Will Be Loved. Then Adam Levine became a TV judge and the band became a hit factory.

name: Mastodon
description: Progressive sludge metal. Leviathan is Moby Dick as a concept album. Crack the Skye goes to space. Blood Mountain splits the difference.

name: Megan Thee Stallion
description: Hot Girl Summer. Savage remix with Beyoncé. Houston rap with a degree. Body-ody-ody.

name: Melanie Martinez
description: Cry Baby is a concept album about childhood trauma dressed in pastels. PORTALS goes alien. Directed her own film. Auteur pop.

name: MGMT
description: Kids. Time to Pretend. Electric Feel. Oracular Spectacular was the blog-era bible. Then they made three deliberately weird albums.

name: Missy Elliott
description: Get Ur Freak On. Work It. Timbaland beats and Missy's vision. Most innovative woman in hip hop history. The rain, supa dupa fly.

name: Mumford & Sons
description: The Cave. Little Lion Man. Banjo folk-rock that filled arenas. Sigh No More started a whole genre wave.

name: Nick Cave
description: The Bad Seeds. Red Right Hand. Into My Arms. Australian gothic. Murder ballads and love songs from the same dark pen.

name: No Doubt
description: Gwen Stefani before solo. Don't Speak. Tragic Kingdom. Ska-punk meets new wave. Just a Girl was the anthem.

name: Olivia Rodrigo
description: Drivers License broke the internet. SOUR is a breakup album that sold 15 million. GUTS proved it wasn't a fluke.

name: One Direction
description: What Makes You Beautiful. Five lads from X Factor. Biggest boy band since the Beatles comparisons. Then Harry went solo.

name: OneRepublic
description: Apologize with Timbaland. Counting Stars. Ryan Tedder writes hits for everyone else too. Pop-rock for the streaming era.

name: Panic! at the Disco
description: I Write Sins Not Tragedies. Brendon Urie's voice and theatrical excess. A Fever You Can't Sweat Out is emo-pop perfection.

name: Patti Smith
description: Horses. Gloria reimagined. Punk poet laureate. Because the Night with Springsteen. Merged poetry and rock before anyone else.

name: Peso Pluma
description: Génesis blew up corridos tumbados worldwide. Ella Baila Sola was inescapable. Mexico's new generation in one voice.

name: Pharrell Williams
description: Half of The Neptunes. Produced half your playlist. Happy became inescapable but G I R L is the real album.

name: Phil Collins
description: In the Air Tonight. That drum fill. Genesis drummer gone solo megastar. Against All Odds. Sussudio. Owned the 80s.

name: PinkPantheress
description: 2-minute songs. TikTok bedroom pop. Boy's a Liar Pt. 2 with Ice Spice. Jungle and drum'n'bass nostalgia from Gen Z.

name: PJ Harvey
description: Rid of Me. Polly Jean shreds, whispers, screams. Let England Shake won the Mercury Prize. Reinvents herself every album.

name: Portishead
description: Dummy invented trip-hop. Glory Box. Sour Times. Beth Gibbons' voice over scratchy vinyl beats. Third went industrial and disappeared.

name: Post Malone
description: Congratulations. White Iverson. Genre? Yes. Beer pong to billion streams pipeline.

name: Procol Harum
description: A Whiter Shade of Pale. That organ intro is one of the most recognizable in rock. Then 12 more albums nobody talks about.

name: Rauw Alejandro
description: Puerto Rican R&B meets reggaeton. Todo de Ti is the hit. Cosa Nuestra goes full salsa. Latin pop's smoothest operator.

name: Roxy Music
description: Bryan Ferry's sophistication meets Brian Eno's chaos. Avalon is the smoothest album ever made. For Your Pleasure is the weird one.

name: Sabrina Carpenter
description: Espresso. Disney kid to pop star pipeline done right. Short n' Sweet is the summer album. Nonsense outros are the secret weapon.

name: Siouxsie and the Banshees
description: Juju. Spellbound. Hong Kong Garden. Gothic post-punk royalty. Siouxsie Sioux's voice cuts through everything.

name: Slayyyter
description: Hyperpop provocateur. Troubled Paradise is Y2K pop maximalism. Daddy AF. Internet pop with zero filter.

name: Squid
description: Brighton post-punk. Bright Green Field is chaotic and brainy. Narrator is seven minutes of controlled panic.

name: Supertramp
description: Breakfast in America. The Logical Song. Crime of the Century. Art-pop disguised as radio rock. That Wurlitzer sound.

name: Swedish House Mafia
description: Don't You Worry Child. Three DJs who broke EDM into the mainstream. Save the World. One reunion tour at a time.

name: Tems
description: Nigerian Afrobeats meets R&B. Free Mind. Featured on Drake and Future, then Born in the Wild proved she doesn't need features.

name: The Killers
description: Mr. Brightside never left the UK charts. Brandon Flowers brought Las Vegas drama to indie rock.

name: The Mamas & the Papas
description: California Dreamin'. Monday Monday. Laurel Canyon harmonies. Four voices that defined the 60s folk-pop sound.

name: The Offspring
description: Self Esteem. Come Out and Play. Pretty Fly for a White Guy. Smash sold 11 million on Epitaph. 90s punk-pop kings.

name: The Stooges
description: Iggy Pop invented punk before punk existed. I Wanna Be Your Dog. Raw Power. Fun House is a noise masterpiece.

name: The Verve
description: Bitter Sweet Symphony. That string sample. Richard Ashcroft walking down the street. Urban Hymns is Britpop's last great album.

name: Thin Lizzy
description: The Boys Are Back in Town. Phil Lynott. Twin guitar harmonies. Jailbreak is the album. Irish rock's greatest.

name: Tito Puente
description: El Rey del Timbal. Oye Como Va before Santana covered it. Dance Mania. 100+ albums. The king of Latin jazz.

name: TLC
description: Waterfalls. No Scrubs. CrazySexyCool sold 23 million. T-Boz, Left Eye, Chilli. Best-selling girl group in American history.

name: Turnstile
description: Baltimore hardcore meets summer vibes. GLOW ON broke hardcore out of basements. Mystery is the crossover.

name: twenty one pilots
description: Stressed Out. Ride. Heathens. Tyler Joseph and Josh Dun turned anxiety into arena anthems. Blurryface went diamond.

name: Village People
description: Y.M.C.A. Macho Man. Disco camp perfection. Every wedding, every sporting event. They knew exactly what they were doing.

name: Wet Leg
description: Chaise Longue. Isle of Wight duo with deadpan humor and guitar hooks. The debut won a Brit Award. Moisturizer keeps it going.

name: Yeah Yeah Yeahs
description: Maps. Karen O's feral energy. Zero is the glam hit. Fever to Tell is art-punk perfection. NYC's wildest.

name: Yeat
description: Twizzy. Flëw. Autonomous trap. Lyfestyle with Drake. The sound of rap's algorithmic future. Tonka.

name: Young Thug
description: Rewrote the rules of rap melody. So Much Fun is the thesis statement. Then they locked him up.
eric_take: young thug while my grandma talks / you cant full appreciate young thug without lyrics mannn / hang up the phone with grandma turn on the young thug

name: Zach Bryan
description: Something in the Orange. Oklahoma poet. DeAnn was recorded in a barracks. Country's most authentic voice in a decade.

name: Zedd
description: Clarity with Foxes. Stay with Alessia Cara. Russian-German producer who brought EDM to pop radio.

name: Flea
description: a plee from flee

name: John Frusciante
description: RHCP's secret weapon, solo catalog deeper than most careers. Niandra LaDes is raw nerve. Curtains is gorgeous. Shadows Collide With People is the masterpiece nobody heard.
